title: buteraea rufescens
intro: Buteraea rufescens, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a plant species that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of saponins, ruscogenins, and ruscinic acid, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Buteraea rufescens include reducing symptoms of varicose veins and improving blood circulation, as well as showing potential in treating conditions such as edema and hemorrhoids. Herbal preparations of Buteraea rufescens include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be obtained from commercial suppliers or grown at home for personal use.
